title: The Volunteer.
content:
"The clashing of my armor in my ears
    Sounds like a passing bell; my buckler puts me
    In mind of a bier; this, my broadsword, a pickaxe
    To dig my grave."
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Hood, titled "The Volunteer.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
